content: We can borrow the human language learning logic of reflexion (as a deliberate vocabulary and critical thinking practice taught in high school English curricula) to redesign LLM self-reflection mechanisms, framing structured, multi-perspective LLM self-reflection as a guided 'learning exercise' rather than an emergent correction step, where the LLM repeats the practice of identifying inconsistencies across its own outputs just as students repeat reflection on their writing to refine clarity and reasoning. This structured exercise-based reflexion can improve LLM self-reflection consistency far more than unprompted internal correction.
